title: Beginner Tactics: Forks, Pins & Back Rank Mates
description: The essential tactical patterns every improving chess player must know. Covers forks, pins, back-rank mates, and skewers with clear examples.

intro.text: Welcome to the Chess99 Beginner Tactical Trainer. If you're playing at the 800-1400 level, you are losing most of your games to simple one or two-move tactics. This video will show you the four patterns you must master first.

segments.title: Pattern 1: The Fork
segments.text: A fork attacks two pieces at once. Your opponent can only save one. Knights are the best forkers because they jump over pieces and attack in a unique L-shape.

segments.title: Beginner Fork Example
commentary: White plays Nxe5, winning a pawn and threatening a fork.
If Black recaptures with the knight, Qh5 gives check and also attacks the knight.
Black cannot meet both threats. White wins material.

segments.title: Pattern 2: The Pin
segments.text: A pin attacks a piece that is shielding a more valuable piece. If the shield moves, the valuable piece is captured. An absolute pin on the king means the piece literally cannot move.

segments.title: Pattern 3: Back Rank Mate
segments.text: When the king is trapped behind its own pawns, a rook or queen can deliver checkmate on the back rank. Always check: can your opponent's rook or queen slide to my back rank?

segments.title: Back Rank Mate Example
commentary: White's rook slides to the eighth rank.
The black king is trapped behind its own pawns — checkmate!
Always look for this pattern when your opponent has not created a flight square for their king.

segments.title: Pattern 4: The Skewer
segments.text: A skewer is like a reversed pin. You attack a valuable piece and when it moves, you capture the less valuable piece behind it. Rooks, bishops and queens deliver skewers.

Master these four patterns and your rating will climb quickly. Practice them with 500 beginner puzzles on the Chess99 Tactical Trainer at chess99.com — each puzzle is from the real Lichess database at your level.
